input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` won't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when you swap the merged dictionary:

```xml
<!-- Instead of this -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- Use this -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

**2. Activity restart on system dark mode change.**
Android recreates the Activity on `uiMode` configuration changes. In your `MainActivity.cs`, add `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges`:

```csharp
[Activity(Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme", MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                  | ConfigChanges.UiMode | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                  | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the change itself, avoiding the full restart. You can then detect the system change and swap your dictionary accordingly:

```csharp
Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    // e.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark or AppTheme.Light
    ApplyTheme(e.RequestedTheme);
};
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource`, and add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to your Activity attribute.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in their .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — This is a common mistake. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time. To get runtime updates when a ResourceDictionary changes, they need to use `DynamicResource` instead.

2. **App restarts when Android dark mode toggles** — By default, Android recreates the Activity when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. In .NET MAUI, you can prevent this by adding `ConfigChanges` flags to the `Activity` attribute to handle the configuration change yourself without restarting.


This is straightforward enough that I can give them a direct answer without needing to examine their code.
